Interpretation: Uses historical rolling backtesting residuals to create lower and upper bounds around the Hybrid SARIMAX + RF forecast.
The conformal margin is calculated from absolute forecast errors observed during rolling validation.
The interval is designed to cover approximately this percentage of future observations under similar data conditions.
Number of rolling validation residuals used to calibrate the interval.
Finite-sample conformal quantile level using the higher quantile rule.
This amount is subtracted from and added to the hybrid forecast to create the prediction interval.
Percentage of final holdout actual values that fall inside the conformal interval.
Average distance between the lower and upper prediction bounds.
Average absolute error of the Hybrid SARIMAX + RF point forecast in the final holdout window.
Managers can use the lower and upper bounds to plan staffing, preparation, and inventory with a demand range instead of one single point forecast.
